symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged high-volume unilateral epistaxis unresponsive to first aid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume and duration beyond a self-limiting bleed, especially against a coagulopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor, lethargy and postural dizziness (early hypovolaemia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic signs of significant blood loss; drive emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known inherited Factor XI deficiency (bleeding diathesis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired stable-clot formation converts an ordinarily minor bleed into an emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood tracking down the posterior pharynx and being swallowed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Posterior blood flow raises airway-soiling and vomiting risk and hides true blood loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 135) with delayed capillary refill (3 s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective compensatory signs of hypovolaemia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers compromise from pallor/dizziness, not a measured pulse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 90/55 mmHg (low-normal for a compensating child)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A near-normal BP is a compensation trap in children and must not be read as reassuring; sealed clinician measurement.
BP is low-normal, excluding a hypertensive arterial bleed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active anterior venous ooze from Kiesselbach's plexus on ENT exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation localises an anterior source — a clinician exam finding, not obtainable via chat.
A visible bleeding vessel without an obstructing object argues against a foreign body.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systolic murmur consistent with known pulmonary valve stenosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fixed outflow obstruction limits compensatory cardiac output — auscultation is clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coagulation studies / Factor XI level and full blood count

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies the deficiency and blood loss and guides factor replacement — hospital investigation, never available in telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Uncontrolled haemorrhage with early shock in a coagulopathic child
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe epistaxis with early hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these three define a time-critical haemorrhagic emergency demanding immediate escalation, regardless of any single 'normal-looking' vital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify blood loss and screen platelets — hospital, on arrival; not orderable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Coagulation profile and Factor XI level
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms severity of the deficiency and guides factor replacement/FFP — specialist haematology, in hospital.
Prepares for transfusion if bleeding continues or haemoglobin falls — emergency-department action.
